summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined several recent developments in small UAS operations and industrial activity. Ukraine has unveiled multiple new systems, including the Gnome-NS ground “mother drone” for carrying FPVs, the Gnom-ND wheeled-tracked FPV carrier drone, and the Ptashka strike quadcopter, which flies on a glass thread invisible to Russian forces. The I-SEE system has been introduced to track Russian drones in real time without emitting radio signals, and the Gretz XL electronic warfare system has been updated to protect against Mavic drones. On the industrial side, Motor-G is now shipping 200,000 drone motors per month, making it Ukraine’s largest engine manufacturer, though there are emerging shortages of fiber optics affecting production. In operational news, Ukrainian forces have knocked out a Russian Kasta-2E2 radar in Zaporizhzhia using FPV drones, and a Russian FPV strike in Orikhiv injured a 67-year-old civilian.
